question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Callback payer confusion where tokens are pulled from the wrong party or an explicit payer can be bypassed'] [Function: take (Midnight.sol) payer = buyerCallback, sellerAssets to receiver] Can a taker of a sell offer (offer.buy=false) set takerCallback=attackerCallback and receiver=attackerAddress, causing safeTransferFrom(loanToken, attackerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) and safeTransferFrom(loanToken, attackerCallback, attackerAddress, sellerAssets) to both pull from attackerCallback, where attackerCallback is a contract that the attacker has induced to hold tokens and approve Midnight, effectively draining the induced contract's full buyerAssets in a single take? Proof idea: fuzz receiver = attacker, takerCallback = victim contract with approval, assert victim contract's tokens are drained.

Proof idea: Foundry stateful fuzz with ghost tracking of allowed payers, assert no unauthorized transferFrom src.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Callback payer confusion where tokens are pulled from the wrong party or an explicit payer can be bypassed'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized + take payer = callback, callback is EcrecoverAuthorizer itself] Can an attacker set the buyerCallback or repayCallback to the EcrecoverAuthorizer contract address, causing onBuy or onRepay to be called on EcrecoverAuthorizer (which does not implement these interfaces and will revert), and if EcrecoverAuthorizer has a standing loanToken approval to Midnight, cause a revert that prevents the take/repay from completing while the nonce state is already updated? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Callback payer confusion where tokens are pulled from the wrong party or an explicit payer can be bypassed'] [Function: take (Midnight.sol) payer = buyerCallback, ERC777 hook] Can a taker use an ERC777-compatible loanToken where safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) triggers a tokensToSend hook on buyerCallback, allowing buyerCallback to reenter Midnight during the token transfer (after onBuy has completed but during transferFrom), observing a state where credit is updated but the second transferFrom (to receiver) has not yet occurred, and exploiting this partial state? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 loanToken with tokensToSend hook, assert no partial state exploitation during transferFrom.
